body:
{
  "title": "1:1 Seren Voss + Casey Tanaka — 2026-09-18",
  "compiled_truth": "Weekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CEO) and [Casey Tanaka](people/casey-tanaka) (Head of Engineering) held on September 18, 2026. Meeting ran about 40 minutes, longer than usual. Primary topic was the growing tension between the roadmap commitments for Q4 and the engineering team's capacity after two back-to-back sprints on the single-pass pipeline hardening work.\n\nCasey opened by flagging burnout signals on the core systems team — three engineers had quietly dropped their Slack activity to near-zero outside working hours, which Casey noted is usually a leading indicator. Seren acknowledged this and asked whether the timeline for the deterministic authentication module could be pushed by two weeks without jeopardizing the early-access commitments [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) made to its first design partners. Casey said yes, with caveats: the integration test suite would need to be descoped slightly, and the team would need to skip the internal demo originally planned for October 1st.\n\nThey also discussed hiring. Casey wants to open two more mid-level infrastructure roles, ideally people with experience in high-throughput systems or codec-adjacent work. Seren pushed back gently, noting that headcount approval from the board isn't guaranteed until after the next financing conversation, which is tentatively scheduled for late October. They agreed to draft the job descriptions now and hold them in reserve. Casey mentioned a referral from a former collegue — someone currently at a mid-stage networking company — and Seren said to move fast on that one informally.\n\nThere was a brief sidebar about documentation debt. Casey expressed frustration that internal docs for the compression primitive interfaces are outdated, which is slowing onboarding for the two engineers who joined in August. Seren suggested dedicating a half-day \"doc sprint\" the following Friday, and Casey agreed to organize it.\n\nFinally, Seren brought up the question of whether Casey should present at the upcoming infrastructure meetup in late October. Casey was hesitant — prefers to stay heads-down — but Seren argued it would help with recruiting visibility for Anchor. They left it as a maybe, with Seren promising to handle the logistics if Casey agrees to do a 15-minute talk.\n\nOverall tone was collaborative but tense around capacity. Both aligned on priorities; disagreement was mostly about pacing.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-08-22** | [Casey Tanaka](people/casey-tanaka) raises initial concerns about sprint pacing in engineering all-hands.",
    "- **2026-09-04** | Two new engineers onboarded to [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) core systems team, triggering documentation gap discussions.",
    "- **2026-09-11** | Previous 1:1 between Seren and Casey; first mention of Q4 roadmap pressure.",
    "- **2026-09-18** | This meeting. Agreed to two-week timeline shift on auth module; deferred headcount decision pending board input.",
    "- **2026-09-25** | Doc sprint held; Casey organizes half-day session to update compression primitive interface docs.",
    "- **2026-10-03** | [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) confirms Casey will present at October infrastructure meetup.",
    "- **2026-10-17** | Casey's referral candidate enters informal interview loop at Anchor.",
    "- **2026-10-29** | Casey gives 15-minute talk on deterministic data infrastructure patterns at local meetup; receives positive reception.",
    "- **2026-11-06** | Board greenlights two additional engineering hires; job descriptions published same week."
  ]
}
